top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on coastal rolling, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Cape Town Cycle Tour's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
67 miles on coastal rolling burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Cape Town Cycle Tour's coastal rolling forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 67 miles.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on coastal rolling. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
58ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Cape Town Cycle Tour. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Cape Town Cycle Tour, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,937ft of climbing on coastal rolling does to your power file on race day.
At Cape Town Cycle Tour, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
5-second power closes gaps and wins field sprints. At Cape Town Cycle Tour, neuromuscular snap is a tactical tool, not just a finish-line move.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Cape Town Cycle Tour’s 67 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 67 miles of coastal rolling in Grand Parade, Cape Town → Green Point.
